Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandamus (Ccp 1094.5)
Off Calendar Feb-24-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Petitioner Debra Abney's Motion For Writ Of Mandamus (CCP 1094.5).

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner's "motion for writ of mandamus" as to the fourth cause of action is denied without prejudice to petitioner bringing a motion that would entirely dispose of the petition, which contains four causes of action. A hearing on a petition for writ of mandamus is the functional equivalent of a trial and results in a final judgment. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1094.5(f) ["The court shall enter judgment either commanding respondent to set aside the order or decision, or denying the writ."]; Code Civ. Proc. sec. 577 ["A judgment is the final determination of the rights of the parties in an action or proceeding."]; see also Code Civ. Proc. secs. 1090 [on petition for writ of traditional mandamus, if return raises a question of fact, the court may order the question to be tried before a jury], 1092 ["The motion for new trial must be made in the court in which the issue of fact is tried"].) Just as a court will not hold a trial on one cause of action of a multi-cause of action complaint without a bifurcation order, the Court will not entertain a piecemeal adjudication of the petition.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
